name: discord-qdrant-indexing-performance-optimization-indexing-cpu-throttling
prompt: Our self-hosted Qdrant machines run on AWS (EC2 c6i.2xlarge with 8 vCPUs and 16GB RAM). We've noticed high CPU usage during indexing, which makes sense since max_indexing_threads is set to 0, meaning it uses all available CPU power. However, we'd prefer to avoid full CPU saturation so that reads and writes aren't affected. The API suggests keeping max_indexing_threads between 8 and 16 to avoid "inefficient or broken HNSW graphs", and that "fewer threads are used on smaller CPUs". Given our setup, what value would you recommend?

rubric.text: Clarifies that 'inefficient' refers only to HNSW build time, not to the quality or correctness of the resulting HNSW graph
States the core tradeoff: reducing threads/CPU allocated to indexing will make HNSW building/indexing take longer, with no way around that tradeoff
Does not claim a fixed golden-rule thread count; recommends picking a value (e.g. 4) and empirically testing performance
Recommends setting optimizer_cpu_budget to -4 as the preferred lever, explaining it limits HNSW building across the entire peer (all collections) and is more resilient to CPU size changes
Mentions max_optimization_threads as a fallback option that can be set per collection, if peer-level configuration access isn't available

Suggests time-of-day scheduling as an alternative: disabling indexing during peak hours via high optimizer thresholds and enabling it at night (e.g. via a cron job), to avoid CPU contention entirely during business hours
Recommends a specific thread count (e.g. 'use 8' or 'use 12') as a universally correct answer without framing it as something to test
Conflates max_indexing_threads with optimizer_cpu_budget or max_optimization_threads as if they are the same setting at the same scope
